headline: DRYDEN GOLD Drills 4.25m @ 32.87 g/t Au Including 0.50m Of 252 g/t — Three New Zones Discovered At Gold Rock
snippet: Dryden Gold (DRY:TSX-V) hit 4.25m @ 32.87 g/t Au including 0.50m of 252 g/t at the new Sparrow zone — one of three simultaneous new discoveries 300m northeast of known high-grade zones at Gold Rock, Ontario. The Ruby and Buccaneer zones also returned strong results in the same drill program.

headline: FRANCE PULLS All Gold From NY Federal Reserve — Books €13 Billion Gain, European Repatriation Trend Begins
snippet: The Banque de France sold 129 tonnes held at the NY Fed and repurchased equivalent bars in Europe, booking a €13B capital gain. France's full 2,437-tonne gold reserve is now held in Paris — and other European central banks are reportedly watching the playbook closely.

headline: ZIJIN To Buy Allied Gold For C$5.5 Billion — Shareholders Approve, Chinese Giant Adds Three African Mines
snippet: Zijin Gold International is paying C$44/share (C$5.5B, ~US$4B) for Allied Gold's Sadiola, Bonikro-Agbaou, and Kurmuk open-pit gold mines across Africa — its largest acquisition ever. Allied shareholders approved the deal April 1; close is expected before the end of April 2026.

headline: Founders Metals Makes FIRST DISCOVERY At Antino North — Hole 1 Returns 17.22 g/t Au Over 3.6m
snippet: The first-ever drillhole at Founders Metals' Antino North concession hit 17.22 g/t Au over 3.6m within 24.6m of 2.81 g/t — a brand-new zone 20km SW of the main Antino deposit in Suriname. Twelve parallel shear targets have been mapped over km-scale on the concession, none previously drilled.

headline: Goldman Sachs REAFFIRMS $5,400 Gold Target — Says Structural Buyers 'Have Not Left' After March Selloff
snippet: Goldman raised its 2026 year-end gold target to $5,400/oz (from $4,900) in January and reaffirmed it following gold's steep March pullback — its worst monthly decline since June 2013. Analysts Struyven and Thomas argue that central banks, macro funds, and fiscal-risk hedgers are structural buyers who will not exit.

headline: China Imports 790+ Tonnes Of Silver In Just 2 Months — SIXTH Straight Year Of Supply Deficit Forecast
snippet: China pulled in over 790 tonnes of silver in January-February 2026 — an eight-year high — driven by solar manufacturers and retail investors pivoting from gold. The Silver Institute forecasts a 67M-oz supply deficit in 2026, extending a structural shortfall now entering its sixth consecutive year.

headline: COPPER Hits $13,225/Tonne Record High — AI Data Centers Now Projected To Consume 475,000 MT In 2026
snippet: LME three-month copper surged 1.8% to $13,225/tonne as supply disruptions and US trade policy uncertainty collided with surging AI infrastructure demand. Data centers alone could consume 475,000 MT of copper in 2026, per J.P. Morgan — which forecasts $12,500/mt in Q2 and ~$12,075/mt for the full year.

headline: Uranium Touched $101/lb Before Pullback — Cameco Sees 'Wall Of Demand' Through 2045 That 'Cannot Be Avoided'
snippet: Uranium spot hit $101.41/lb on January 29 before retreating to $85.50 after geopolitical volatility. Cameco says uncovered utility requirements to 2045 are at record levels, while Sprott Physical Uranium Trust purchased 5M+ lbs year-to-date and the US DOE committed $2.7B for domestic enrichment capacity.

headline: Coeur Mining Closes New Gold Deal — Creates $20 Billion All-North-American Producer, Gold Output Up 80%
snippet: Coeur Mining (CDE:TSX/NYSE) completed its all-share acquisition of New Gold on March 20, adding New Afton and Rainy River to its portfolio and lifting 2026 gold guidance to 680,000–815,000 oz — roughly 80% more than pre-deal. Combined market cap stands at approximately $20 billion.

headline: Core Lithium RESTARTS Finniss Mine After 2-Year Shutdown — Energy Storage Demand Surges 55% In 2026
snippet: Australian producer Core Lithium is restarting its Finniss lithium mine — mothballed since 2024 on depressed prices — as recovering spodumene markets and a China-driven energy storage boom change the math. Lithium demand for grid-scale battery storage is forecast to grow 55% in 2026, following a 71% jump in 2025.

headline: Mining M&A Hits 13-Year High At $93.7 Billion — Mega-Deals Up 68%, Gold Dominates At 77% Of Volume
snippet: Global mining M&A totalled $93.7B in 2025 — the highest yearly value since 2012 — with billion-dollar mega-deals up 68% from 2024. Gold and silver assets captured 77% of deal volume; January 2026 continued the pace with $11B in transactions and US projects taking 48% of new capital allocation.

headline: Gold Miners: RECORD MARGINS, Compressed Multiples — Analysts Say Equities Still Haven't Caught Up To $4,700 Gold
snippet: As Q2 2026 opens, gold producers are generating record free cash flow but institutional investors remain structurally underweight the sector. Kinross was upgraded to Outperform with a $45 target (implying 11%+ FCF yield); Agnico Eagle is top pick among majors — and analysts increasingly argue the equity catch-up trade is overdue.

headline: Troilus Mining (TLG) Launches 40,000m 2026 Drill Campaign At Quebec Copper-Gold Project
snippet: Troilus Mining (TLG:TSX) is drilling 40,000m in 2026 at its 435 km² Quebec copper-gold project, targeting underground resource growth below the Z87 pit, high-grade continuity down plunge, and the new Waubimo multi-element anomaly 4km south of Sumitomo's Regnault deposit.

headline: Central Banks Added RECORD 700+ Tonnes Of Gold In 2025 — PBOC Buys For 16 Straight Months Heading Into 2026
snippet: Central banks added over 700 metric tonnes of gold to reserves in 2025 — the highest annual total since 1967 — and the PBOC has purchased gold for 16 consecutive months. Analysts forecast 755–850 tonnes of sovereign buying in 2026, providing a structural floor under the $4,700+ gold price.
